title: roldana glinophylla
intro: Roldana glinophylla, also known as Mexican mint marigold, is a perennial herb native to Mexico and the southwestern United States, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and pungent, minty aroma. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Roldana glinophylla include its ability to aid digestion, reduce stress and anxiety, and provide relief from menstrual cramps, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to cater to individual needs and preferences.
